summary: Ship the command built-in kind — the skill surface's legacy file placement: a lone `.claude/commands/*.md` file, the skill's field schema by import, file-stem identity, both channels

new.description: Discovery folds `.claude/commands/*.md` into command members with file-stem identity and the skill's field schema; a command sits on both invocation channels.

edit.description: Add `claude_code_command()` — Governs root `.claude/commands`, glob `*.md`, `UnitShape::File` (file-stem identity), `Format::YamlFrontmatter`, the skill's Extraction primitives by reuse, registration = {user-invoked, description-trigger}; add to `all_kinds()` (line 109) and the `definitions`/enumeration tests (lines 271-286).

Add the `command` kind reusing the `Skill` field schema (locus `.claude/commands/*.md`, file-stem identity, both channels) + `commandFloor` = the skill's documented recommendations, cited to code.claude.com/docs/en/skills (2026-07-07).
Regenerate — the command kind's declaration + floor rows join the lock (four kinds).
Assert the command kind's declaration row re-derives (four kinds).

tests.asserts: a `.claude/commands/deploy.md` file folds into a command member with id `deploy` (file stem) and the skill's field schema; the command registers on both invocation channels

acceptance: The command kind ships as the skill surface's second placement: `.claude/commands/*.md` files fold into command members (file-stem identity, skill schema by import, both channels), the built-in lock re-derives byte-clean, and `commandFloor` carries the skill's documented recommendations (builtins.md, 'The shipped kinds').
notes: 0014, command kind. Second placement of the skill surface, never a second schema (commands merged into skills, code.claude.com/docs/en/skills 2026-07-07 per 0014). File-stem identity already exists (rule); no src/kind.rs change. 0014 leaves unmodeled: subdir namespacing, personal commands dir, name charset. Blocked on REGISTRATION-CHANNELS (needs the two-channel set; shares builtin_kind.rs, builtins.ts, builtin_lock.toml). scoped at 89cd964.

summary: Ship the agent built-in kind + named-field identity (the third mode): `.claude/agents/**/*.md`, id from the frontmatter `name` field, name+description required, the docs' name charset

new.description: Discovery folds `.claude/agents/**/*.md` (recursively) into agent members whose id is the frontmatter `name`, not the filename; the charset clause fires on an out-of-charset `name`; two agents sharing a `name` in one scope trip the uniqueness clause.

edit.description: Add the named-field identity mode — a member's id read from a declared frontmatter field (agent's `name`), the third mode beside file-stem and directory-name (`UnitShape`, lines 106-118).

`Member::from_source` (line 152) derives the id from the named frontmatter field for the named-field mode, not the file stem/directory name; error when the field is absent (the id-absent error variant, lines ~100-110).

Add `claude_code_agent()` — Governs root `.claude/agents`, glob `**/*.md` (recursive, subdirs organizational), named-field identity on `name`, `Format::YamlFrontmatter`, `name`+`description` Field primitives, registration = {description-trigger}; add to `all_kinds()` (line 109) + enumeration tests (five kinds).
Spell the named-field identity mode in the `KindFacts`/`UnitShape` surface (the id source, not just `identityField`'s projection detail, lines ~62-66).

Add the `agent` kind (locus `.claude/agents/**/*.md`, named-field identity, name+description) + `agentFloor`: required fields, `name` charset via `allowed_chars` ('lowercase letters and hyphens'), any closed vocabularies the docs document, per-scope name uniqueness — each cited to code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents (2026-07-07).
Regenerate — the agent kind's declaration + floor rows join the lock (five kinds).
Assert the agent kind + its floor clauses re-derive (five kinds).
Cover the agent kind + agentFloor (required fields, charset, uniqueness).

tests.asserts: an agent's id is its frontmatter `name` (a file `foo.md` with `name: my-agent` → id `my-agent`); a subdirectory is organizational; an uppercase `name` trips the charset clause; duplicate `name`s in one scope trip uniqueness

acceptance: The agent kind ships: `.claude/agents/**/*.md` files fold recursively into agent members identified by the frontmatter `name` (the third identity mode), the built-in lock re-derives byte-clean, and `agentFloor` gates required fields, the documented `name` charset, and per-scope name uniqueness — each clause cited to the sub-agents doc (builtins.md, 'The shipped kinds').
notes: 0014, agent kind + named-field identity — the third mode, real engine work in Member::from_source. Charset 'lowercase + hyphens' + required name/description cited to code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents (2026-07-07 per 0014). Closed-vocab/uniqueness clauses encoded only where the doc documents them decidably (else dropped, honest encoding). Plugin-context variants deferred (0014). Blocked on COMMAND-KIND (shares builtin_kind.rs, builtins.ts, builtin_lock.toml). scoped at 89cd964.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.4, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. No `.github/workflows/release.yml` exists (only the `temper.yml` check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest.

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
